title: sapindus saponaria subsp. drummondii
intro: Sapindus saponaria subsp. drummondii, also known as the American soapbark or soapberry tree, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the Sapindaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to produce a soap-like substance called saponin. The medicinal properties of Sapindus saponaria subsp. drummondii have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or salve, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat wounds, burns, and other skin irritations.
